name: Suspicious Debugger Registration Detected
description: Detection of a suspicious debugger registration for a program available on the Windows logon screen. Adversaries can register a debugger to a sticky key application (e.g., `sethc.exe`), allowing them to launch a command prompt with system-level privileges from the logon screen. This is a common defense evasion and privilege escalation technique.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a suspicious debugger registration was detected for a sticky key application. This is a strong indicator of **Defense Evasion** and **Privilege Escalation** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1027.004**), where an adversary is attempting to create a backdoor that can be launched from the logon screen. The command line was **{command}** and the event occurred under the user **{user}**. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious registry entry. This event should be treated as a confirmed security incident until proven otherwise.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line that was executed to register the debugger.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
